nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; typically found near steep drop-offs and caves at depths of 200–400 m, with seasonal migrations into shallow coral-reef waters on moonless nights.

diet.notes: Naturally feeds on zooplankton, small crustaceans, and other small invertebrates, using bioluminescent light organs to detect prey in darkness. In captivity, has been reported to accept mysis shrimp, krill, and other meaty foods, but typically only after lights-out; several small feedings per day are generally advised.

summary: A peaceful nocturnal reef fish that produces light from symbiotic bacteria housed in rotating organs beneath each eye. Generally regarded as a specialist species best kept in small groups in dimly lit, expert-level aquariums.
careNotes: Bioluminescent organs beneath each eye host symbiotic Vibrio bacteria that emit a steady glow; the fish can rotate the organ to flash the light on and off, using it for intraspecific signaling, prey detection, and predator evasion. Generally considered strictly nocturnal — hides during the day inside caves or overhangs and only emerges to feed after lights-out, so the display should provide ample shaded rockwork, deep caves, and subdued lighting (or a moonlight-only photoperiod). Best kept in a small group (three or more) with other nocturnal species; loners tend to do poorly. Reported to be extremely sensitive to copper-based medications and should never be exposed to them. RESOLVED 2026-06-03: Adult size disagreement resolved. LiveAquaria's 9 in (~23 cm) figure is an outlier; FishBase (35 cm TL), Wikipedia (35 cm TL max, average 10–30 cm), and BlueZoo Aquatics (~36 cm / 14 in) all agree on a biological maximum near 35 cm. adultSizeCm updated from 20–30 to 15–35 cm to match the FishBase/Wikipedia consensus — the lower bound reflects the small end of the Wikipedia average range, the upper bound reflects the confirmed wild maximum. Captive specimens are typically reported smaller (10–25 cm) but planning to the biological maximum is appropriate for a specialist exhibit. Tank-size guidance varies between sources (LiveAquaria 125 gal / ~473 L; BlueZoo 150 gal / ~568 L; Reeflex 2000 L); the 1000 L value is retained as a conservative specialist recommendation suited to a nocturnal schooling group requiring substantial dark refugia.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary source. Provided care level, diet, water parameters, minimum tank size, temperament, reef-compatibility, copper sensitivity. The collection landing URL supplied by the discovery agent did not list this species; the canonical product page was located by search and used in its place.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperament, native range, diet, care level, reef-compatibility. Reported adult size 14 in (~36 cm), agreeing with FishBase/Wikipedia wild maximum but disagreeing with LiveAquaria's 9 in.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Taxonomy (family Anomalopidae, order Trachichthyiformes), max length 35 cm TL, preferred temperature 17.9–27.8 °C (mean 24.1 °C), distribution, depth, diet (zooplankton).

additional.name: Wikipedia (Splitfin flashlightfish)
additional.notes: Confirmed max size 35 cm, nocturnal schooling behavior, distribution from Philippines/Indonesia to Tuamotus, light-organ rotation mechanism.

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Provided specialist tank-size guidance (2000 L minimum), temperature range 22–27 °C, and confirmation of strictly nocturnal habit; identifies the species as suitable only for advanced aquarists.
